      The bailey has the stone outlines of various castle buildings, kitchens, stable, chapel, all in the green grass.
pages.notes: This page continues directly from A_0080_R which described Conisborough Castle (Oct 1992). The full sentence at top right reads clearly: 'The bailey has the stone outlines of various castle buildings, kitchens, stable, chapel, all in the green grass.' The left margin contains a column of partial words/fragments (e.g. 'Su', '& a', 'outs', 'collo', 'than', 'choi', 'pre', 'on', 'the', 'sys', 'us', 'a l', 'delic', 'of n.', 'bed', 'look', 'the', 'ymp') that are the left-side portions of lines of text cut off at or obscured by the binding gutter. These fragments cannot be fully read and are transcribed as partial. No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner. Year context is 1992 based on prior spread.

pages.full_text: [photo: family group of adults and children eating at a folding table outside a white camper van on a grassy campsite, trees in background]
pages.notes: The right page consists almost entirely of a glued-in colour photograph showing a family eating at a camping table outside a white camper van (appears to be a Talbot Ducato/similar) on a grassy campsite with trees behind. No written caption is visible. The top-left corner shows the gutter overlap text 'tlines' and 'ss.' from the left page sentence about the bailey. No handwritten page number visible. The photo content is consistent with a UK camping trip, likely 1992 context from prior spread.
